top_stories.title: The Back Door Attackers Know About — and Most Security Teams Still Haven’t Closed
top_stories.summary: The article examines persistent OAuth tokens created by third-party app integrations with Google and Microsoft as a security vulnerability largely overlooked by enterprise security teams. These tokens have no expiration dates, bypass MFA and perimeter controls, and can be exploited by attackers without requiring user credentials.

top_stories.title: Securing the Agentic Workforce: Cisco Announces Intent to Acquire Astrix Security
top_stories.summary: Cisco has announced its intent to acquire Astrix Security, a company specializing in securing non-human identities and AI agents. The acquisition is aimed at extending Zero Trust principles to agentic workforces, addressing the growing challenge of managing and securing machine identities and AI-driven entities in enterprise environments.

top_stories.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
top_stories.summary: A critical authentication-bypass vulnerability in cPanel has been disclosed, with multiple proof-of-concept exploits rapidly emerging in the wild. A researcher claims zero-day exploitation activity has been occurring for at least a month prior to public disclosure, putting millions of cPanel-based systems at risk.

top_stories.title: Microsoft Details Phishing Campaign Targeting 35,000 Users Across 26 Countries
top_stories.summary: Microsoft disclosed a large-scale phishing campaign observed between April 14-16, 2026, that targeted over 35,000 users across 13,000 organizations in 26 countries. The attack used code of conduct-themed lures combined with legitimate email services to redirect users to attacker-controlled domains for authentication token theft.

top_stories.title: Progress Patches Critical MOVEit Automation Bug Enabling Authentication Bypass
top_stories.summary: Progress Software has released security patches for MOVEit Automation addressing two vulnerabilities, including a critical authentication bypass flaw. MOVEit Automation is an enterprise managed file transfer solution, and the authentication bypass vulnerability represents a significant access control risk for organizations using the product.

news.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
news.summary: A proof-of-concept exploit demonstrates that Microsoft Edge stores passwords in process memory, allowing extraction by someone with administrative privileges. The vulnerability poses credential theft risks in enterprise environments where browser-based password storage is used.

news.title: CloudZ malware abuses Microsoft Phone Link to steal SMS and OTPs
news.summary: A new CloudZ RAT variant deploys a malicious plugin called Pheno that hijacks Microsoft Phone Link to steal SMS messages and one-time passwords from mobile devices. The attack targets OTPs, which are commonly used in MFA workflows, by exploiting the legitimate PC-to-phone mirroring application.

news.title: AWS IAM now provides higher maximum quotas for roles, role trust policies, instance profiles, managed policies, and identity providers
news.summary: AWS IAM has increased maximum quotas for six resources, including doubling customer managed policies per account from 5,000 to 10,000 and instance profiles per account from 5,000 to 10,000. Managed policies per role limits were also increased, along with other IAM resource quotas affecting roles, role trust policies, and identity providers.

news.title: Workload Identity Federation
news.summary: Anthropic has introduced Workload Identity Federation (WIF) for the Claude API, allowing workloads to authenticate using short-lived identity tokens from existing identity providers instead of static API keys. The feature uses OpenID Connect (OIDC) tokens, eliminating the need for long-lived credentials in machine-to-machine authentication scenarios.

news.title: Breaking the code: Multi-stage ‘code of conduct’ phishing campaign leads to AiTM token compromise
news.summary: Microsoft Defender Research identified a large-scale phishing campaign using code of conduct-themed lures to conduct adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) attacks that steal session tokens rather than just credentials. The multi-stage attack chain leverages legitimate email services to bypass email authentication and ultimately compromises authenticated session tokens, circumventing MFA protections.

news.title: Cybercrime Groups Using Vishing and SSO Abuse in Rapid SaaS Extortion Attacks
news.summary: Two cybercrime groups, Cordial Spider and Snarky Spider, are conducting rapid SaaS extortion attacks that abuse SSO (Single Sign-On) systems alongside vishing (voice phishing) techniques. The attacks involve high-speed data theft while operating within SaaS environments and leaving minimal forensic traces, with researchers attributing specific tactics to each cluster.

news.title: Poisoned Ruby Gems and Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines for Credential Theft
news.summary: A software supply chain attack campaign using malicious Ruby gems and Go modules has been attributed to the GitHub account "BufferZoneCorp," targeting CI pipelines. The attack enables credential theft, GitHub Actions tampering, and SSH persistence through sleeper packages that later push malicious payloads.

news.title: IAM Roles Anywhere now enforces VPC endpoint policies for the CreateSession API
news.summary: AWS IAM Roles Anywhere now supports VPC endpoint policy enforcement for the CreateSession API. This update allows administrators to configure allow or deny rules on the CreateSession API through VPC endpoint policies, providing more granular network-level access control.

news.title: IAM tools help Oracle Red Bull Racing keep pace with strict F1 regulations
news.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing implemented 1Password's identity technology to improve efficiency in aerodynamics testing procedures that must comply with strict F1 regulations. The deployment addressed identity and access management needs within a highly regulated competitive environment.

news.title: Govern your bots carefully or chaos could ensue
news.summary: Gartner projects that Global Fortune 500 enterprises will run more than 150,000 AI agents by 2028, up from fewer than 15 today, raising significant governance concerns. Without proper governance frameworks, the rapid proliferation of AI agents presents identity and access management challenges around authorization, accountability, and access control for non-human identities.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of cybercrime group Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted to conducting SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign operated without deploying malware, relying solely on token theft to silently compromise authentication credentials at scale.
